passages.text: 71. Geologists, contrary to normal working folks, believe that knowledge  of the concepts of geology can help to find petroleum. Some petroleum explorationists  still  do  not  admit  to  a  need  for geologists to aid them in their search. In 1975 a well-known oil  researcher from Dallas, Texas was determined in not allowing geologists join his  team, because he believed that they were too expensive and decreased  his  profits per barrel significantly. These examples are not isolated cases, and it  has been argued that oil may better be found by  random  drilling  than  by  the appliance of scientific principles. Petroleum geology is only  one  aspect  of  petroleum  exploration  and production.
